compiled_state.title: Write failing tests for integration test fixture setup (setup-fixtures.sh)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description
Create tests/integration/test-fixture-setup.sh with behavioral tests for setup-fixtures.sh. Uses mock git clone (fake git binary in temp dir). Tests must fail (RED) before setup-fixtures.sh exists.

### Tests:
1. test_creates_fixture_dir: After setup-fixtures.sh runs, tests/integration/fixtures/ directory exists.
2. test_fixture_for_python_add_parameter_present: After setup, fixtures/python-add-parameter/ directory exists with expected structure.
3. test_fixture_for_typescript_add_parameter_present: fixtures/typescript-add-parameter/ exists.
4. test_fixture_for_python_normalize_imports_present: fixtures/python-normalize-imports/ exists.
5. test_skips_existing_fixtures: When fixture dir already exists and is non-empty, setup-fixtures.sh skips the clone (idempotent). Verify by checking mock git clone was NOT called again.
6. test_graceful_skip_on_network_failure: When git clone fails (mock returns exit 1), setup-fixtures.sh exits with message "Fixture download failed — integration tests will be skipped" and exits 0 (non-blocking for CI).
7. test_fixture_integrity_check: setup-fixtures.sh verifies each fixture has a test runner (e.g., pyproject.toml or package.json) present; outputs warning if fixture is corrupt.

Also create tests/integration/ directory. Add .test-index entry: plugins/dso/scripts/integration-fixtures/setup-fixtures.sh (or wherever it lives): tests/integration/test-fixture-setup.sh

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/integration/test-fixture-setup.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/integration/test-fixture-setup.sh
- [ ] Valid bash syntax
  Verify: bash -n tests/integration/test-fixture-setup.sh
- [ ] Tests fail in RED phase
  Verify: bash tests/integration/test-fixture-setup.sh 2>&1; [ $? -ne 0 ]
